blocks.heading: The deterministic game engine and the multi-season loop — the physics of the whole simulation.

rows.value: Intra-game hockey resolved shift-by-shift, the between-game and offseason machinery (injuries, cap, contracts, draft, free agency, awards), and the season orchestration that ties it together.
It transforms world state into outcomes — stats, injuries, events — that every downstream domain reacts to. It is the one place game rules live.
A full game resolves in **<2 ms** so thousands of seasons can run for calibration. That performance bar shapes the whole design.

bigIdea.label: The load-bearing idea
bigIdea.sub: Players live in a **struct-of-arrays** (`RosterSOA` — parallel typed arrays), the hot loop pre-allocates its scratch buffers, and every random draw comes from a seeded `createRng`. Same seed → same game, every time — which is exactly what makes replays and the calibration harness possible.

blocks.title: The pieces
blocks.intro: A small hot core (game, shift, roster layout) wrapped by orchestration (season, offseason) and a set of leaf systems (contracts, events, key moments). Tap a card to jump to its full section.

cards.name: sim-game
cards.purpose: The per-game entry: initializes game state, runs three periods (plus OT and shootout if tied), and finalizes a `SimResult`.

cards.name: sim-shift
cards.purpose: The hot loop, ~120× per game. Picks lines, computes matchups and fatigue, and rolls events — shots, hits, penalties, injuries — as attribute-driven probabilities.

cards.name: sim-season
cards.purpose: Walks the schedule day by day, threads between-game state (fatigue recovery, injury advance, chemistry), and fires the brain triggers.

cards.name: roster-soa
cards.purpose: The performance heart: a struct-of-arrays player layout (parallel typed arrays for 30+ attributes), loaded once per team for cache-friendly, allocation-free simulation.

cards.name: contracts/
cards.purpose: The contract engine: ELCs, extensions, buyouts, qualifying offers, three-team trades, and salary bargaining sessions.

cards.name: events/ · km/
cards.purpose: The boundary to the story engine: a season-event bus (firings, retirements, succession) and the Key-Moment yield hook that surfaces in-game decision points.

blocks.title: How a season runs
blocks.intro: `simSeason` is the orchestrator. For each game it calls `simGame`, which drives ~120 `simShift` calls; post-game reconciles standings and injuries, then the day's brain triggers fire.

blocks.title: The pieces, in depth
blocks.intro: A full section per piece — what it does, how it works, its files and exports, and how it connects to the rest of the engine.

components.name: sim-game
detail: `simGame(context)` is the per-game entry. It builds the `GameState` and RNG, pre-allocates scratch buffers and a batch of RNG values, computes team-level offense and pace, then runs three periods of 40 shifts each. If the score is tied it runs 3-on-3 OT and, if needed, a shootout, before `finalizeCGResult` rolls up per-player stats into a `SimResult`. The whole call is the first box of the [season loop](#arch) above.

files.name: sim-game.ts
files.desc: Game init, the period / OT / shootout loop, and result finalization.

exports.name: GameContext · SimResult
exports.desc: The input (roster, lines, coach, seed) and output (events, stats, injuries).

components.name: sim-shift
detail: The hot loop, ~30 seconds of game time per call. It picks lines (the away team disadvantaged by last change), computes a shift context (line offense quality, pace, fatigue and zone-start bias, momentum), updates time-on-ice, then rolls events as probabilities. Every event — a scoring chance and its shot/save resolution, hits, penalties, takeaways, fights, injuries — is a base rate scaled by the relevant attributes. There are **no allocations** here; that is the <2 ms budget being defended.

files.name: sim-shift.ts
files.desc: Line picking, shift context, and the `rollEvents` probability cascade.

exports.name: simShift()
exports.desc: Resolves one ~30s shift, mutating game state in place.

connections.body: the game event stream (shots, goals, hits, penalties, injuries)

components.name: sim-season
detail: `simSeason` walks the schedule day by day: it runs each game, reconciles standings and injuries post-game, then runs a daily tick (fatigue recovery, injury advance, chemistry) and fires the brain triggers. `sim-multi-season` wraps it with the offseason — playoffs, awards, retirements, draft, free agency, development, and the identity season tick — and emits season events to the story engine. The full path is the [spine](atlas.html#spine) on the atlas.

files.name: sim-season.ts
files.desc: The regular-season loop and between-game state threading.

files.name: sim-multi-season.ts
files.desc: Multi-season orchestration: playoffs, awards, offseason, succession.

files.name: daily-tick.ts · post-game.ts
files.desc: Fatigue / injury recovery and standings / injury reconciliation.

exports.name: PreparedTeam · SeasonGameResult
exports.desc: The per-team prepared state and per-game result shapes.

connections.body: `PreparedTeam` state — fatigue, injuries, chemistry between games

components.name: roster-soa
detail: Rather than an array of player objects, a team's roster is a **struct-of-arrays**: one parallel typed array per attribute. The hot loop indexes by player slot, which keeps memory access cache-friendly and avoids per-shift allocation — the single biggest reason a game fits in the time budget. `loadRosterSOA` converts `GeneratedPlayer[]` into this layout once and sorts players by role (NHL skaters, NHL goalies, then AHL).
components.codeHtml:
interface RosterSOA {
  shooting:   Float32Array;  // one slot per player
  skating:    Float32Array;
  fatigue:    Float32Array;
  confidence: Float32Array;
  // …30+ skater fields, 10 goalie fields, all parallel
}

files.name: roster-soa.ts
files.desc: The struct-of-arrays layout and its accessors.

exports.name: RosterSOA · loadRosterSOA()
exports.desc: The layout type and its loader.

exports.name: createRng() · nextGaussian()
exports.desc: The deterministic RNG every system draws from.

connections.body: [sim-shift](#c-sim-shift) every shift, by player slot index

components.name: contracts/
detail: A ~22-file engine for everything money. `computeCapState` derives ceiling, committed hit, dead cap, LTIR, and projections; on top of it sit the negotiation surfaces — a triplet engine for three-team trades, an auction for asset ordering, and a bargaining session for salary/term — each backed by comparable-player indices. Entry-level deals, extensions, qualifying offers, and buyouts round it out.

connections.body: the offseason market — free agency, waivers, re-signings

components.name: events/ · km/
detail: The narrow boundary to the narrative layer. `events/season-events.ts` is a season-boundary bus — a discriminated union of `CoachFiredEvent`, `GMRetiredEvent`, `PlayerRetiredEvent`, `OwnerSucceededEvent`, … — that the orchestrator emits after personnel changes. `km/yield.ts` samples Key-Moment opportunities by tier (cinematic / standard / background) for the story engine to resolve.

files.name: events/season-events.ts
files.desc: The season-boundary event bus and its event union.

exports.name: emit()
exports.desc: Publishes a season event after a personnel change.

connections.body: [story](domain-story.html#c-director) — the season-event bus and KM opportunities

blocks.title: Data it owns
blocks.intro: The engine produces outcomes and the per-game state to compute them; it borrows the schema from `world` and attributes from `profiles`.

rows.name: SimResult · SimSeasonResult
rows.desc: The output of a game / season: event stream, player stats, injuries, and final results.

rows.name: GameState · LineState · PlayerInGameState
rows.desc: In-memory per-game state: score, penalties, period, on-ice lines, per-player TOI & stats.

rows.name: RosterSOA
rows.desc: The struct-of-arrays roster — parallel typed arrays for every skater & goalie attribute.

rows.name: SkaterSeasonStats · GoalieSeasonStats
rows.desc: Accumulated season totals, rolled up from per-game stats.

rows.name: CapState
rows.desc: Computed salary-cap state: ceiling, committed hit, dead cap, LTIR, projections.

blocks.note: Reads (never owns): `League`/`Game`/`Player` schema from `lib/world`; `SkaterSkills`, `CoachProfile`, personality from `lib/profiles`.

blocks.title: Seams
blocks.intro: A narrow, data-oriented surface in; decisions and events out.
blocks.note: Between games it calls `brain` triggers to get decisions, then executes them; it emits season events and Key-Moment opportunities to `story`. The dependency on intelligence/narrative is by *callback*, not control — `sim` stays the driver.
